mechanism_narrative: A three-month rise in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A 3-month rise in INDPRO does not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or rank information coefficient falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5% indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days while the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.5% while consumer prices are rising, the resulting negative real interest rate forces capital out of money-market instruments into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 217.729 in March 2010
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a bivariate logistic regression has a p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals reviving consumer demand and corporate top-line momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363194 million dollars in February 2010
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery periods, positive month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 58% of weeks, or the regime-specific rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets and accelerating household income growth, which boosts discretionary spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Three-month declines in UNRATE do not precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the walk-forward rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strength in residential investment and construction activity, which drives broad economic multiplier effects and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 575 thousand units in February 2010
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST fail to precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or the mean forward return during positive HOUST months is not significantly greater than during negative HOUST months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above 2.0% annualized indicates aggregate demand outperformance that supports upward earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days following the GDP release.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth exceeding 2.0% annualized do not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean forward return in high-growth quarters is not significantly greater than in low-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, overwhelming earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 48% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
